title: Boston Just Built a 47-Foot Soccer Ball and Called It a World Record
script: A 47-foot soccer ball? Boston just shattered a Guinness World Record. Massport — the Massachusetts Port Authority — inflated a 47.9-foot diameter soccer ball at Piers Park II on the East Boston waterfront this week. The previous record was a 39.98-foot ball from Qatar in 2013. This one is bigger by nearly 8 feet. The new ball towers over Boston Harbor and can be seen from the water. It was made by Britten Inc. in Michigan, measured by professional surveyors, and officially certified by Guinness on Friday. Why? FIFA World Cup 2026 comes to Boston this summer. Today is technically the last day it sits in the park. If you're near East Boston — go now. So: is an inflatable world record the most Boston thing Boston has ever done for the World Cup?
